heading: Stabilization assistance for Poland
label: 5412 Stabilization assistance for Poland

children.text: should work closely with the European Community and international financial institutions to determine the extent of emergency assistance required by Poland for the fourth quarter of 1989, and

should consider extending a bridge loan to relieve immediate and urgent balance of payments requirements using the Exchange Stabilization Fund in accordance with paragraph (1)(A) of .section 5411(b) of this title

children.heading: Immediate, multilateral response to Poland’s economic stabilization needs
children.label: b Immediate, multilateral response to Poland’s economic stabilization needs
children.text: In furtherance of , the President, acting in coordination with the European Community, should seek to ensure that the industrialized democracies undertake an immediate, multilateral effort to respond to Poland’s request for $1,000,000,000 to support its economic stabilization program.section 5411(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Additional authority to provide stabilization assistance
children.label: c Additional authority to provide stabilization assistance

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 1 Authority
children.text: In order to carry out paragraph (1) of , the President is authorized to furnish assistance for Poland, notwithstanding any other provision of law, to assist in the urgent stabilization of the Polish economy and ultimately to promote longer-term economic growth and stability, based on movement toward free market principles. Such assistance may be provided for balance of payments support (including commodity import programs), support for private sector development, or for other activities to further efforts to develop a free market-oriented economy in Poland.section 5411(b) of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For purposes of providing the assistance authorized by this subsection, there are authorized to be appropriated $200,000,000 for fiscal year 1990 to carry out chapter 4 of part II of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( and following; relating to the economic support fund), in addition to amounts otherwise available for such purposes.22 U.S.C. 2346
